title: Updates available but manual intervention required

Try `lake update` and then investigate why this update causes the lean build to fail.

title: Correct find_equation_id Python script for tautological equations

body:
- Return an id of 0 for tautological equations other than Equation 1
- Avoid wrong Equation.id results when the equation is non-canonical

title: Bump actions/cache from 4 to 5
author: dependabot[bot]
body:
Bumps [actions/cache](https://github.com/actions/cache) from 4 to 5.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/cache's releases.

v5.0.0

[!IMPORTANT] actions/cache@v5 runs on the Node.js 24 runtime and requires a minimum Actions Runner version of 2.327.1.

If you are using self-hosted runners, ensure they are updated before upgrading.

What's Changed

  • Upgrade to use node24 by @​salmanmkc in actions/cache#1630
  • Prepare v5.0.0 release by @​salmanmkc in actions/cache#1684

Full Changelog:

title: Prepare arxiv v2
author: blefloch
body:
- I've had to implement a listings language definition from scratch instead of relying on the one provided in the Lean repository, because of uncertainty on whether Apache-licensed code could be uploaded in our CC-BY-licensed article.
- The paper now compiles equally well in pdfLaTeX, XeLaTeX, LuaLaTeX, to avoid complaints by some coauthor.
- Minute last-minute layout tweaks.

title: Use listings instead of minted
author: blefloch
body:
The minted package uses external tools to parse the code listings, which leads to difficulties with the arXiv.  The listings package is less flexible but good enough for our purposes.

This PR includes `lstlean.tex` from https://github.com/leanprover/lean4/blob/master/doc/latex/lstlean.tex

title: [PAPER] Keep `_minted` directory
author: marcusrossel
body:
Discussion: https://leanprover.zulipchat.com/#narrow/channel/458659-Equational/topic/Submitting.20the.20paper/near/563803579

To use `minted` on the Arxiv, one has to include the `_minted` cache directory ([reference](https://info.arxiv.org/help/faq/texlive.html#changes-2025-minted)) which is generated while building the PDF. 
A subsequent PR should automatically keep this directory updated as part of CI.

title: Paper dec13 improvements
author: blefloch
body:
Mostly cosmetic changes (e.g., improve tables), fixing LaTeX problems, and fixing very minor mistakes except:
- More details on magma sizes as a function of order in Table 1 (without taking more space vertically)
- Add Douglas McNeil's ORCID
- Use `\date{December 2025}` instead of `\date{\today}`
- Normalize dates to DD Month YYYY format

title: [PAPER] Fix a name in author list
author: zaklogician
body: This short PR  changes my name in the article author list and metadata to the form I use in all my other academic work.

title: [PAPER] Small changes: figures and thanks
author: JoseBrox
body:
- Changed figure file names in Section 7.3. Figure redone to remove anomalous spikes. Figure width changed to get better alignment.
- Added acknowledgement to computational resources.

title: fix: prevent explorer from displaying any data for tautological equations, and improve error display
author: Osalotioman
body:
This PR adds a check to the equation explorer for tautological equations by checking if the shape and rhyme on the lhs and rhs of the equation are the same, except for equation1[x=x]. It also removes stack trace from the error pop up in the explorer, but leaves it in the console instead. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3f165d6e-1275-463d-ae7b-54f82ed2cec3 This resolves #1411
Info on use of Number In lhsRhyme and rhsRhyme number is used for the slice method since it does not support bigInt: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Array/slice
The code could be re-written to somehow support shapeOrder value which exceeds JavaScripts Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER (2**53), but a string with the order of the shape on the rhs or lhs exceeding 2*53 will have a stri

title: separate vdash and models in blueprint
author: teorth

title: Tautological equations are parsed incorrectly by the Equation Explorer
author: blefloch
body:
**Describe the bug**
On the Equation Explorer entering a tautological equation other than `x=x` results in displaying the equation that would follow that tautological equation.

**To Reproduce**
1. Go to https://teorth.github.io/equational_theories/implications/
2. In the first text box, type `x*(y*x)=x*(y*x)`
3. Click `Jump to Equation`
4. Observe that the equation displayed is the non-trivial [Equation 4314](https://teorth.github.io/equational_theories/implications/?4314) `x◇(y◇x) = x◇(y◇y)`.

**Expected behavior**
Either a message stating that the equation is tautological, or silently pointing to https://teorth.github.io/equational_theories/implications/?1 would be better than showing an equation that is different from the one that was input.

title: Let the Equation Explorer parse equations again (see #1409)
author: blefloch

title: The Equation Explorer fails to parse any equation
author: blefloch
body: On the page https://teorth.github.io/equational_theories/implications/ , clicking on the "Jump to Equation" button after typing any equation such as `x=y` or `x*y=y*z` or even wrong ones such as `x*y` or `x=y=z`, always produces the error `Oops! There's an issue with your equation Error: Your equation should have exactly one '=' sign. Please check your input.` I'm submitting an attempted fix shortly.

title: Enlarge the Equation Explorer figure, avoid bad page breaks
author: blefloch
body: This should only be merged after Osalotioman's PR #1402 It makes sure that page breaks stay reasonable despite the height change of Figure 16.  For the appendices I added \raggedbottom, which has a very minor effect since the appendices contain almost no flexible spaces.  The main effect is to get rid of warnings. I also added Kisielewicz's first name to a reference to reduce an underfull hbox's badness.
